description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese katana sword accompanied by its matching scabbard. The blade is long, gently curved, and single-edged, with a dark, polished finish that suggests high-quality steel. The handle is wrapped, likely in ray skin and cord, and features a decorative end cap and a circular guard (tsuba) with metallic accents. The scabbard is lacquered, predominantly black with gold or brass embellishments and subtle patterning along its length. The overall craftsmanship and proportions indicate it is both functional and ornamental, suitable for display or ceremonial use. The sword and scabbard together present a striking contrast of dark and metallic tones, with visible attention to detail in the fittings.
description: A traditional Japanese katana sword with a curved, polished blade, ornate handle, and matching black-and-gold scabbard. The sword features a wrapped grip, circular guard, and decorative metallic accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the katana and its scabbard, highlighting the blade's curve and the scabbard's gold embellishments.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the circular guard and the alignment of the handle and blade, with the scabbard above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the other face of the blade and scabbard, with similar decorative features visible.
description_view_3: Reverse end-on view, focusing on the handle's end cap and the thin profile of the blade and scabbard.
attribute_handle_design: wrapped, cylindrical handle with cord and decorative end cap
attribute_ornamentation: gold/brass embellishments and subtle patterns on scabbard and fittings
